heroPoems.title: Clouded by Three Currents
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Clarity, frenzy, inertia—
three currents,
one ocean.
heroPoems.tagline: For rooms lit by one lamp.

heroPoems.title: Guard the Heart
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Guard your heart
with all vigilance,
for from it
flow the springs of life.

heroPoems.title: Rhapsody on a Windy Night
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Twelve o'clock.
Along the reaches of the street
Held in a lunar synthesis,
Whispering lunar incantations
Disolve the floors of memory
And all its clear relations,
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

heroPoems.title: The thief left one thing behind
heroPoems.firstStanza:
The thief left one thing behind:
the moon
at my window.

heroPoems.title: Coromandel Fishers
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Rise, brothers, rise; the wakening skies pray to the morning light,
The wind lies asleep in the arms of the dawn like a child that has cried all night.
Come, let us gather our nets from the shore, and set our catamarans free,
To capture the leaping wealth of the tide, for we are the kings of the sea!

heroPoems.title: The Movement of the Way
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Returning is the motion of the Way.
Yielding is the method of the Way.
heroPoems.tagline: For when the street goes quiet.

heroPoems.title: From the street where my beloved lives send fragrance to me
heroPoems.firstStanza:
From the street where my beloved lives send fragrance to me
I am pale with waiting
I am ill with distance
one breath from that doorway would heal me

heroPoems.title: Ask the wound what survives after the blade.
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Ask the wound what survives after the blade.
Ask the scar what survives after the wound.

heroPoems.title: A Litany in Time of Plague
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Adieu, farewell earth's bliss,
This world uncertain is,
Fond are life's lustful joys,
Death proves them all but toys,
None from his darts can fly,
I am sick, I must die:

heroPoems.title: The Run of the Downs
heroPoems.firstStanza:
_The Weald is good, the Downs are best_--
_I'll give you the run of 'em, East to West._
Beachy Head and Winddoor Hill,
They were once and they are still,
Firle, Mount Caburn and Mount Harry
Go back as far as sums'll carry.

collections.name: The Long Stairwell
collections.tagline: For moments lit by a single lamp.
collections.theme: A thread of attention to rain, glass, paper, and hands at rest.

featuredPoem.title: Intellect and Resolve
featuredPoem.firstLines:
One intellect distinguishes right from wrong.
Another rationalizes appetite.

poems.title: Where is my ruined life and where my noble name

collections.name: Under the Same Lamp
collections.tagline: For when memory leans over your shoulder.
collections.theme: Poems that make waiting feel textured rather than empty.

featuredPoem.title: One-Pointed Intelligence
featuredPoem.firstLines:
A scattered mind buys a thousand roads.
A clear mind walks one—

poems.title: I carried a thousand desires, each one lethal.
This world is a children's playground before me.

collections.name: When Evening Leans In
collections.theme: A muted field of recollection, where detail carries the emotional weight.

featuredPoem.title: Before I got my eye put out
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Before I got my eye put out,
I liked as well to see

poems.title: Hope is the thing with feathers
I had no time to hate, because
